description: A sleeping child or a faint sound may call for quiet. 'shiu' asks the room to settle, but the request itself grants no authority over other speakers.
articulatory_notes: Raise the tongue blade toward the postalveolar area and send unvoiced air through its central groove for 'sh', then lift the tongue into high-front unrounded 'i'. Draw the tongue back and round the lips for high-back 'u' in the second syllable. Stress 'shi'.
sound_symbolism: 'shiu' begins with a sustained 'sh', so the request demonstrates the quieter sound it asks for.
usage_notes: Lengthening the opening fricative can extend the hush while the word remains a request.
